envVars.SENDMUX_API_KEY: Optional Sendmux API key or scoped agent token used by CLI, SDK, HTTP, or MCP examples.
envVars.SENDMUX_ROOT_KEY: Optional Sendmux root key for account-level Management API setup.
envVars.SENDMUX_MBX_KEY: Optional Sendmux mailbox key for Mailbox and send-capable mailbox workflows.
envVars.SENDMUX_BASE_URL: Optional Sendmux API base URL override for CLI examples.
envVars.SENDMUX_MCP_SURFACES: Optional comma-separated surfaces for the combined Sendmux MCP server.
envVars.SENDMUX_MAILBOX_API_KEY: Optional mailbox key for the Mailbox surface in the combined MCP server.
envVars.SENDMUX_MANAGEMENT_API_KEY: Optional root key for the Management surface in the combined MCP server.
envVars.SENDMUX_SENDING_API_KEY: Optional send-capable mailbox key or owner-approved agent token for the Sending MCP surface.
envVars.SENDMUX_MCP_HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN: Optional bearer token expected by the local HTTP MCP server.

sendmux-attachments.description: Move email attachments through Sendmux without putting file bytes in model context, using file paths, presigned URLs, CLI, SDKs, or MCP.

sendmux-cli.description: Use the Sendmux CLI for terminal-driven Management, Mailbox, and Sending workflows with JSON output and scoped credentials.

sendmux-email-for-agents.description: Give OpenClaw agents a Sendmux inbox to receive, triage, route, reply to, and send email with owner approval and scoped credentials.

sendmux-getting-started.description: Set up Sendmux for agents, choose MCP, CLI, SDK, or HTTP, validate scoped credentials, and make the first harmless call.

sendmux-mailbox-agent.description: Let an OpenClaw agent read, search, count, sync, triage, file, delete, thread, and reply from one Sendmux mailbox efficiently.

sendmux-management.description: Manage Sendmux domains, mailboxes, mailbox keys, sending accounts, webhooks, logs, billing, and account-level setup.

sendmux-mcp-setup.description: Connect OpenClaw and other agent clients to hosted or local Sendmux MCP servers for mailbox, sending, and management tools.

sendmux-send-email.description: Send one or many emails through Sendmux using approved mailbox or agent credentials, idempotency keys, attachments, MCP, CLI, SDKs, or HTTP.

sendmux-token-efficient-usage.description: Choose low-token Sendmux calls across MCP, CLI, SDKs, and HTTP by using snippets, counts, batches, deltas, cursors, ETags, and idempotency.
